active_paradigms.idea: Proxy-authoritative read service with LRU-bounded last-commit map
active_paradigms.description: Make StorageProxy the single source of truth for latest committed values since all writes traverse it. Maintain a per-key lastCommittedValue map and serve reads directly at the proxy when there is no inflight write for that key. Implement the map as an LRU to keep metadata compact and evict cold keys. On eStorageWriteResp, update the lastCommittedValue and release any queued reads. On eStorageRead from a CacheNode, check for an inflight write barrier first; if none and the key exists in lastCommittedValue, immediately respond to the caller without touching storage; otherwise, enqueue as usual. This preserves LSI because the proxy only serves from its cache after a storage commit. Keep per-key small state: hasInflightWrite, waitingReaders (for fan-out), and an OrderedDict for the LRU store. This replaces broadcast or lease schemes with on-demand authoritative answers at the proxy, reducing storage round trips under mixed workloads.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize safety_score by only serving from proxy after write commits; improve round_trip_efficiency by eliminating storage hops for cacheable reads; improve cache_hit_rate via LRU reuse of hot keys.
active_paradigms.cautions: Never serve from proxy when there is an inflight write for that key; ensure eMonitorProxyEnqueue/Forward/Hold/ResponseDelivered are still announced correctly for operations that do hit storage; bound memory with LRU while avoiding evicting keys that have queued waiters; maintain request IDs so multiple concurrent readers of the same key are replied correctly and only once.

active_paradigms.idea: Priority proxy scheduler: write-collapsing and write-priority using a min-heap
active_paradigms.description: Install a two-lane scheduler at StorageProxy that prioritizes writes and collapses multiple pending writes for the same key into the last-one-wins before forwarding. Use a heap keyed by (priority, enqueue_seq) where writes get higher priority than reads, and within a key retain only the newest write, canceling older queued writes for that key. For reads, if a newer write is pending for the same key, keep the read queued behind that write; once the write commits, serve the read from the proxy’s lastCommittedValue fast path without hitting storage. This reduces redundant storage writes and read trips during write bursts. Maintain a side map key->latestPendingWriteOpId to allow O(1) overwrite of older entries and prevent duplicates from being forwarded.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Improve round_trip_efficiency by collapsing redundant writes and shifting reads to post-commit proxy-serve; maintain safety_score by serializing per-key and never serving reads across an inflight write barrier; indirectly increase cache_hit_rate by keeping proxy lastCommitted hot for read-after-write.
active_paradigms.cautions: Ensure correctness of collapse: only the newest write per key is forwarded; respond to clients of collapsed writes consistently (e.g., all receive success/fail based on the forwarded write); preserve the proxy’s nondeterministic forward/hold semantics by letting the heap select but still emit eMonitorProxyHold on no-op steps; maintain per-key inflight queues for matching eStorageWriteResp and read fan-out.

active_paradigms.idea: Proxy-stamped per-key commit counters with on-demand validation from pods
active_paradigms.description: Have StorageProxy maintain a per-key monotonically increasing commitCounter that increments on every successful eStorageWriteResp. CacheNodes cache (value, counter) when they see a write response for their own write or after a storage read. On eClientRead, a CacheNode sends a lightweight validate request carrying its cached counter; the proxy compares to its authoritative counter: if equal, it immediately returns the cached value (no storage trip); if higher at the proxy, it replies with the latest value; if a write is inflight, the proxy queues the read until commit, then replies with the fresh value. This gives a safe, single-RTT validation path without needing to push state or compare raw values, and it works even under shard movement because the single proxy serializes all storage traffic.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize safety_score by only accepting equality to the proxy’s authoritative counter; improve round_trip_efficiency by turning many reads into single proxy round trips; increase cache_hit_rate by allowing CacheNodes to reuse their local cached values when counters match.
active_paradigms.cautions: Counters are proxy-local truth; never guess them at CacheNodes. Increment strictly on successful write commits and handle counter creation on first write. For keys with no known counter at proxy or CacheNode, fall back to storage read. Ensure requests carry request IDs and counters are per-key to avoid cross-key contamination. Maintain monitor announcements even for validate-only fast paths (ResponseDelivered with isWrite=false).

tried_paradigms.idea: Lease-holder redirection with epoch leases to enable cache-served reads
tried_paradigms.description: Add a LeaseManager machine that grants per-key epoch leases to at most one CacheNode (lease-holder). Only the lease-holder may write a key; non-holders forward client writes to the holder (inter-pod), which then performs a write-through via the proxy. On successful commit, the holder updates its cache and broadcasts a LeaseCommit(key, value, epoch) to peers; peers mark their cache entries with that epoch. Reads on any pod are served from cache if their local entry’s epoch equals the manager’s latest epoch; otherwise, the CacheNode requests the latest epoch/value from LeaseManager (inter-pod) and updates the cache. Lease transfer/renewal is a constant-sized epoch bump; when a lease changes holder, the manager bumps the epoch, invalidating stale holders. This keeps metadata compact (map key->(holder, epoch, value)) and does not depend on ranges/ownership. Crucially, most reads are satisfied from cache or LeaseManager without touching the proxy, cutting proxy RTTs while guaranteeing LSI by using the lease-holder’s write-through commit path as the authoritative way values change.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Reduce proxy round trips by redirecting reads/writes through lease-holder and LeaseManager instead of storage; keep cache_hit_rate high by ensuring a single hot cache per key holds latest committed data; maintain safety_score via lease epochs and write-through on the holder only.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Ensure only the holder writes; non-holders must never go directly to storage for that key. Do not serve from cache if the local epoch < manager’s epoch. Handle in-flight lease transfers by bumping epoch before granting the new lease, and only committing after the holder’s write completes. Avoid split-brain holders by serializing lease grants per key at LeaseManager.

tried_paradigms.idea: CacheNode-side read de-duplication, write coalescing, and read-on-write futures
tried_paradigms.description: Inside each CacheNode, maintain per-key state to avoid redundant proxy hops: (a) If a read for key K arrives while a read to K is already inflight, queue the client as a waiter and do not send another eStorageRead; fan out the single proxy response to all waiters. (b) If multiple writes to the same key K arrive while a write to K is inflight or pending, overwrite the pending value and suppress additional eStorageWrite sends; when the inflight write completes, if a newer pending value exists, send a single write with only the last value. (c) If a read to key K arrives while this node has a write to K inflight, enqueue the read and serve it from the committed value carried in the eStorageWriteResp when it returns, without issuing a separate read. Keep opIds per key to match responses and a per-key waiter list for reads. This preserves LSI because any read served without a proxy hop waits for a committed write response value or is satisfied by a single storage read shared by many readers; no speculative cache hits are used. Under write-heavy workloads, this significantly reduces cache->proxy round trips by eliminating duplicate reads and collapsing write storms to the latest value per key.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Reduce local pod/cache <-> proxy round trips under contention, improve round_trip_efficiency without sacrificing safety_score, slightly improve perceived cache_hit_rate via read-on-write futures (served locally post-commit).
tried_paradigms.cautions: Never serve from local cache speculatively; only complete queued reads from a write’s committed value returned by eStorageWriteResp or from the single in-flight storage read. Ensure strict per-key FIFO for waiter lists so older reads complete first. Emit eMonitorProxyRequestIssued only for actual sends. Be careful to update and clear per-key inflight flags correctly to avoid starvation; after a write completes, check for a coalesced pending value and send exactly one follow-up write. Match eStorage*Resp by key and the oldest inflight op to remain robust to proxy reordering.

tried_paradigms.idea: Proxy-sourced commit push (ticketless) plus cache serve-on-push only
tried_paradigms.description: Augment the StorageProxy to broadcast a CommitNotice(key, value, version) to all CacheNodes after it receives eStorageWriteResp. Each CacheNode maintains (value, version) per key and a boolean fresh flag that is set only upon receiving CommitNotice for that key. Reads are served locally without touching the proxy only if fresh is true and the node returns exactly the pushed value for that key; otherwise, it falls back to the normal storage read path. The proxy never grants on-demand tokens and CacheNodes never probe; instead, safety is enforced by serving-from-cache only off a push derived from a confirmed storage commit observed by the proxy. This is ticketless and push-driven, so under balanced write-heavy workloads many reads after commits convert to cache hits with zero proxy hops.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Increase cache_hit_rate while preserving safety_score by only enabling cache hits on commit-pushed values; reduce local pod/cache <-> proxy round trips for post-commit reads.
tried_paradigms.cautions: To preserve LSI, never serve from cache unless the value originated from a CommitNotice that was generated strictly after the corresponding storage commit. Clear the fresh flag on any local eviction or upon observing any local write-inflight to that key until the next CommitNotice arrives. Because pushes can be delayed, a CacheNode may transiently miss hits; this is acceptable for safety. Do not add conditional tokens or epochs on demand (differs from conditional validation); the serving rule is pure push-origin only. Still announce eMonitorProxyResponseDelivered for logical push deliveries if required by the harness, and keep the proxy’s storage-queue behavior unchanged for actual storage IO.

tried_paradigms.idea: CacheNode-side batching: per-key write buffering and single-flight read barriers
tried_paradigms.description: Introduce a small per-key write buffer in each CacheNode that delays sending a new write for key K if another write to K is inflight; instead, store the latest value in a pending slot and, when the inflight write completes, immediately send exactly one follow-up write with the final buffered value. For reads, implement a single-flight barrier: if a read for K arrives with no inflight read, send exactly one eStorageRead and attach any subsequent readers to its waiter list until the response comes back; if a write to K is inflight, reads wait for its eStorageWriteResp and complete using that value (no separate read). This converts bursts of operations on the same key into at most one outbound send at a time and exploits natural locality in write-heavy workloads. The protocol remains simple and safe because every value served without a new proxy hop is either the committed value from a just-finished write or the shared response from a single storage read.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Reduce the number of cache->proxy sends per key by collapsing write bursts and coalescing read storms; improve round_trip_efficiency especially under write-heavy contention while keeping safety_score maximal.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Avoid indefinite buffering: if a follow-up write is pending when a write resp arrives, send exactly one new write and clear the buffer; do not chain unlimited deferrals. Ensure waiter lists are drained on both write and read completions. Maintain strict per-key response matching to avoid mixing values across operations. Emit monitoring events only for real sends, and keep fromCache=false for read completions that used a shared storage read; only mark fromCache=true if served from a committed write’s returned value without an extra read.

tried_paradigms.idea: CommitBus publish-subscribe: proxy broadcasts per-key commit watermarks; add a SharedCache behind pods
tried_paradigms.description: Introduce a tiny CommitBus helper machine that the StorageProxy notifies only at two points: when it enqueues a write and when it receives a write response. The bus maintains, per key, a compact (lastCommitCsn, lastValue, hasOutstandingWrite) tuple derived strictly from proxy-observed storage acks. CacheNodes subscribe to the bus (single broadcast channel), keep a mirror of the watermark map, and can serve reads locally without touching storage only if (a) hasOutstandingWrite is false for the key and (b) their cached value-tag equals the bus’s lastCommitCsn. To improve hit rate under write-heavy load, add one SharedCache helper machine (pod-facing, not storage-facing) that also subscribes to the bus and stores (key -> (value, csn)); CacheNodes first query SharedCache; on hit where csn matches bus watermark, they respond locally; on miss, they fall back to proxy. This preserves LSI because only commit-ack-derived watermarks authorize cache returns; it scales since metadata is per-key counters and a single pub-sub channel. It is robust to key-range/ownership moves because keys are opaque and authority is the commit watermark at the proxy layer.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize safety_score via strict commit-ack-derived tags; increase round_trip_efficiency by serving hot reads from SharedCache and local caches; increase cache_hit_rate by centralizing hot values and broadcasting commits promptly.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Keep StorageProxy network-centric: only emit two lightweight notifications to CommitBus and do not short-circuit storage reads; ensure per-key request IDs and opId matching remain unchanged. Guarantee that CacheNodes NEVER serve from cache if bus.hasOutstandingWrite[key] is true or if their cached tag != bus.lastCommitCsn[key]. Announce eMonitorProxy* events unchanged. Ensure broadcasts are asynchronous but monotonic: apply updates in order and guard against reordering with csn checks.

tried_paradigms.idea: Proxy fast-path If-Equal validation: CacheNode sends a value-hint; proxy replies immediately if it matches last committed
tried_paradigms.description: Extend eStorageRead with an optional hintValue and hintHas flag from CacheNode to StorageProxy. StorageProxy maintains a minimal per-key mirror of the last committed value only by observing storage read/write responses (no speculative state). On receiving eStorageRead, if hintHas is true and there is no queued write for that key and hintValue equals the proxy’s lastCommittedValue[key], the proxy immediately returns eStorageReadResp to the caller (announce eMonitorProxyResponseDelivered) without touching storage. Otherwise, it enqueues and forwards to storage as today. This preserves LSI because the proxy’s equality check is against values learned from actual commit/read acks, and it refuses fast-path when any write for the key is pending. It avoids epochs/leases and keeps authority metadata compact (one scalar per key). Under balanced write-heavy workloads with temporal locality, many reads can be satisfied via this equality-validated fast-path, reducing round trips.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize round_trip_efficiency by enabling zero-storage-touch reads on equality match; maintain safety_score by only using commit-ack-learned values and gating on no in-flight writes; modestly increase cache_hit_rate by letting CacheNodes leverage their local data as hints.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Do not let the proxy cache mutate application semantics: it must only mirror lastCommittedValue on storage responses and must not fabricate values. Carefully gate on per-key pending writes in the proxy queue; if any exist, disable the fast-path. Preserve strict request-ID matching; update proxy inflightPerKey logic to prevent mixing fast-path and queued ops. Keep all monitoring announcements intact and ensure the optimization does not bypass eMonitorProxy* emission requirements.

tried_paradigms.idea: Peer-cooperative cache with commit-counters: proxy stamps write acks with per-key commit counters; pods gossip committed values
tried_paradigms.description: Have StorageProxy attach a monotonic per-key commitCounter to each eStorageWriteResp it forwards back to the issuing CacheNode (counter increments only on successful write responses). CacheNodes store (value, commitCounter) per key. Additionally, introduce a lightweight peer-to-peer Gossip machine: after receiving a committed write, the writer pod asynchronously gossips (key, value, commitCounter) to its peer CacheNode(s). A CacheNode may serve a read from its local cache without storage touch only if (a) it has a cached entry and (b) it has not observed, via any later gossiped tuple or local write ack, a higher commitCounter for that key; practically, maintain lastSeenCounter[key] and serve only when cache.counter == lastSeenCounter[key]. This preserves LSI because only commit-ack-stamped counters authorize reads, and any later commit (from any pod) will be learned via gossip before serving the next read. It reduces round trips under write-heavy, cross-pod workloads because peers quickly converge on latest committed values without hitting storage or proxy for each read.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Boost round_trip_efficiency by avoiding proxy on post-commit reads across pods; maintain safety_score by requiring equality to lastSeen commitCounter; improve cache_hit_rate via rapid dissemination of committed values.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Ensure commitCounter is generated and advanced exclusively at the proxy on successful write responses; do not attempt to estimate or reorder at pods. Gossip is best-effort but must be applied monotonically (ignore stale tuples). Never serve from cache if there is any local knowledge of a higher counter (including pending local writes). Maintain request-ID integrity and all proxy monitor events unchanged.

tried_paradigms.idea: Proxy-centered directory with invalidate-before-write (synchronous acks) enabling zero-RTT cache reads
tried_paradigms.description: Make StorageProxy a directory for per-key cache coherence. On eStorageWrite enqueue, the proxy first sends eInv(k, invId) to all CacheNodes holding key k in Shared state and waits for eInvAck(invId) from each, then forwards the write to storage. After eStorageWriteResp, the proxy multicasts eCommitUpdate(k, value, ver) so caches can install the committed value and enter Shared state. CacheNode serves eClientRead from local cache only when its per-key state is Shared and no local invalidation is pending (i.e., it has not seen eInv for that key without having acked). Reads then require 0 trips to proxy and 0 storage access, yet satisfy LSI because proxy never allows a write to commit until all prior Shared replicas have been invalidated. This is not a lease/ownership scheme; there is no primary pod and no timeouts, just a synchronous invalidate barrier before each write. Maintain per-key directory membership at the proxy compactly as a bitset/set of pods, and announce monitor events unchanged for storage-bound steps.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: safety_score first (strict barrier before writes), then round_trip_efficiency by making reads zero-RTT to proxy and zero storage trips; cache_hit_rate by keeping keys in Shared after commit pushes
tried_paradigms.cautions: Rely only on single-sender FIFO from proxy to each cache: send all eCommitUpdate before any subsequent eInv to the same cache to keep ordering simple. Do not proceed to storage until all invalidation acks are received; otherwise LSI can be violated. Ensure that all write traffic still goes through the proxy queue and that eMonitorProxy* announcements are preserved for real storage sends/responses. Use explicit invId/reqId to match acks, and handle races where a read arrives concurrently with an invalidate: serving the old value is safe because the write has not been forwarded to storage until after ack.

tried_paradigms.idea: Quiescent-watermark push from proxy plus 1-RTT metadata-only validation fallback
tried_paradigms.description: Have the proxy maintain per-key lastCommittedSeq and a pendingWrites counter. After dequeuing a write and upon receiving its eStorageWriteResp, increment lastCommittedSeq(k) and push eCommitUpdate(k, value, seq) to caches. When the per-key pendingWrites counter drops to zero, also push eQuiescent(k, seq=lastCommittedSeq(k)). A CacheNode serves a read from cache immediately if it has (cache.seq == lastCommittedSeq_cached) and has already processed eQuiescent for that seq (i.e., a stable point with no in-flight writes). If uncertain (missing quiescent or sequence mismatch), it issues a lightweight eProxyMetaRead(k) to the proxy (no storage touch) to fetch current lastCommittedSeq; it serves locally if its cache.seq matches, else it falls back to normal storage read via proxy. This preserves LSI because local-serve only happens at known quiescent seqs or after fresh seq confirmation, yet significantly cuts storage trips and often avoids any proxy RT when quiescent has been observed.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Reduce storage reads (round_trip_efficiency) while keeping LSI via quiescent barriers; opportunistically raise cache_hit_rate by serving instantly at quiescent points; safety_score stays maximal by using explicit sequence matches
tried_paradigms.cautions: Ensure proxy emits eCommitUpdate before eQuiescent for the same key and that both come from the same sender to exploit FIFO delivery. Never serve locally on a mere commit update without an accompanying quiescent or confirmed seq, to avoid racing a new write. Keep eProxyMetaRead a pure proxy-local lookup that still triggers eMonitorProxyRequestIssued/ResponseDelivered but does not enqueue to storage.

tried_paradigms.idea: Two-phase windowed commit broadcasting (color epochs) to enable local reads inside stable windows
tried_paradigms.description: Partition time into per-key colored epochs managed by the proxy. The proxy assigns each forwarded write to the current color C(k) and, after all writes of color C(k) commit, broadcasts eColorStable(k, color=C, value=lastValue, ver) to caches and advances C(k) to the next color. Caches mark keys as Stable(color=C) upon receiving eColorStable and can serve reads locally as long as the key remains in that stable color and no eColorAdvance has been received. New writes first trigger eColorAdvance(k, nextColor), flipping caches to Unstable for that key before any storage write is forwarded. This creates clear stable windows where the cache view is provably equal to storage’s latest committed state. Reads during stable windows are 0-RTT to proxy and 0 storage; reads outside use normal proxy/storage path.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Increase periods of safe, zero-RTT local reads (cache_hit_rate, round_trip_efficiency) by batching commits into stable windows; maintain strict correctness by advancing color before forwarding any new writes
tried_paradigms.cautions: All color-control messages (eColorAdvance, eColorStable) must be sent by the proxy and rely on per-sender FIFO to each cache. Do not issue eStorageWrite for a key until eColorAdvance has been sent for that key, preventing local serves from overlapping with new writes. Keep metadata compact: per-key small integer color and a bit indicating Stable vs Unstable. Ensure monitor events still reflect only storage-facing operations.
